cspd.situation: CSPD launched Uplift — a major pedagogical transformation — across 80 schools in August 2025, just as it locked in ~$19m/year in new salary costs and recorded a -$20.6m operating deficit. Patrick Ellis, recruited from CECG where he ran the exact same programme, knows what's missing. The missing pieces — structured curriculum resources, a formative assessment analytics layer, and a teacher workload relief programme — are buildable within 12 months. The first post-Uplift NAPLAN cycle is 2026. That deadline is not soft.
investments: Uplift framework launched August 2025 — system-wide explicit instruction across all 80 schools
Canvas LMS deployed system-wide — already generating rich assessment data
Patrick Ellis hired as EGM Learning Outcomes from CECG — the person who built Catalyst
Frances Waterford elevated to EGM IT (CIO) — technology is now a strategic function
Five-Year Strategic Plan 2024-2029 — Mission, People, Quality, Relevance, Sustainability, Trust
99% EA approval October 2025 — highest staff confidence in the new leadership team

peers.name: CECG — Catalyst
peers.detail: 5,400+ curriculum resources, 75% reading proficiency vs 66% national, teachers save 6 hrs/week. Patrick Ellis built this.

peers.name: MACS — Vision for Instruction
peers.detail: Launched Feb 2024, early-adopter schools showed up to 46 percentage point NAPLAN improvement. One year ahead of CSPD.

peers.name: BCE — AI Copilot
peers.detail: 12,500 educators, 9.3 hrs/week saved per teacher, Year 3 NAPLAN +7.22% while state average fell. Catholic-values-grounded, within a governed environment.

peers.name: CEWA — LEADing Lights
peers.detail: Real-time principal dashboards across 164 schools integrating student, attendance, wellbeing, HR and finance data.

pains.title: Will Uplift actually land before 2026 NAPLAN?
pains.loss: Without a curriculum resource base and evaluation baseline before the 2026 NAPLAN cycle, CSPD will have no way to demonstrate Uplift's impact — and peers will already be publishing data.

pains.title: Can we hold our teachers?
pains.loss: Teacher attrition during the Uplift rollout would be doubly costly — salary costs are locked in at +3%/year while the programme depends on sustained teacher engagement.

pains.title: Will the operating deficit compound?
pains.loss: Each year the Sustainability pillar remains an aspiration rather than a programme, the structural deficit compounds and eventually forces a choice between investment and stability.

recommendations.title: Curriculum Resource Library + External Evaluation
recommendations.detail: Co-develop structured resources for Uplift with CSPD's Canvas team. Establish a Deloitte-style evaluation baseline before the 2026 NAPLAN cycle.

recommendations.title: Principal Analytics Layer on Canvas + CSNSW
recommendations.detail: Surface Canvas formative assessment data into principal dashboards. Incremental build on existing CSNSW Azure infrastructure, not greenfield.

recommendations.title: AI Teacher Workload Programme (Within Domed Policy)
recommendations.who: Frances Waterford + Daniel Lynch, EGM People & Culture
recommendations.detail: Teacher-facing AI pilot modelled on BCE's St Francis trial. Not student-facing. Within the existing governance model.

timing: 2026 NAPLAN cycle — first post-Uplift measurement, baseline must be set before it
Patrick Ellis — six months in post, building his programme now
EA salary commitment — ~$19m/year locked in, efficiency case is now concrete
BCE AI data published August 2025 — the 9.3 hrs/week benchmark is in the market
SCS reporting overhaul announced February 2026 — 12-18 month competitive window
starters: "Patrick, you ran the CECG Catalyst build from 2020. When you walked into CSPD in September, what was the first thing you looked for that wasn't there yet?"
"The 2026 NAPLAN cycle is the first data point that will tell the Board whether Uplift is working. What's the measurement architecture you're putting in place?"
"BCE published results showing 9.3 hours saved per teacher per week through their Copilot pilot — grounded in Catholic values. Has that landed in any conversations here?"
"The Sustainability pillar — is that mostly a capital allocation story, or is there a back-office efficiency programme being scoped alongside?"
"With 500+ families turned away from Westmead every year and the new campus opening in Term 3, what's the plan to convert that waitlist?"
